① Signal
Signal
Play the over 6.5.2 units
The Read
✓Stuff — 32% whiff, changeup 38% putaway, 11.2 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
→Command — 60% FPS, 2.9 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
✓Matchup — NYY K-rate runs +8% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #23/30 vs LHP); whiffs 28% on his changeup — a strong tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
→Volume — 6.0 IP avg — clears 6.5 with ~5.2 IP at his 11.2 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
⚠Projection — 8.8K vs 6.5 (+2.3K), σ2.9, CAP 71%, 10 starts — real edge, but the floor is live (high variance).
→Line — best value at the 6.5 alt line, not the 7.0 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: High-confidence edge — 71% model conviction at 8.8K vs 6.5 line.
Bet wins if
✓NYY whiffs 28% on his changeup — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CH finishes counts (38% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓NYY K-rate runs +8% vs neutral for his mix — more K-prone than average, worth ~0.6K over a neutral draw
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~5.2 IP to reach 6.5, right at his 6.0 IP avg — one short outing and it misses
✗σ2.9K — floor sits near 6K, under 6.5 on an off night

The Read
✓Stuff — 29% whiff, splitter 34% putaway, 10.1 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
✓Command — 65% FPS, 1.4 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
→Matchup — TEX K-rate runs +2% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #10/30 vs RHP); whiffs 37% on his splitter — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
→Volume — 5.8 IP avg — clears 5.5 with ~4.9 IP at his 10.1 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 7.4K vs 5.5 (+1.9K), σ2.3, CAP 70%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: High-confidence edge — 70% model conviction at 7.4K vs 5.5 line.
Bet wins if
✓TEX whiffs 37% on his splitter — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓FS finishes counts (34% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓1.4 BB/9 — no free passes; he attacks and every PA is a K chance
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~4.9 IP to reach 5.5, right at his 5.8 IP avg — one short outing and it misses

① Signal
Signal
Over 4.5 is the play.1 unit
The Read
✓Stuff — 33% whiff, changeup 39% putaway, 10.8 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
⚠Command — 52% FPS — falls behind too often; weak contact tends to follow, not Ks.
→Matchup — CHC K-rate runs -3% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #14/30 vs RHP); whiffs 31% on his changeup — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
→Volume — 4.4 IP avg — clears 4.5 with ~3.7 IP at his 10.8 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 5.7K vs 4.5 (+1.2K), σ1.9, CAP 64%, 9 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 6.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 5.7K vs 4.5. 64% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Bet wins if
✓CHC whiffs 31% on his changeup — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CH finishes counts (39% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~3.7 IP to reach 4.5, right at his 4.4 IP avg — one short outing and it misses

CAP 60% · implied 60.6% (-154) · model 59% (-144) · -1.5% edge
L5.5
-154
① Signal
Signal
Over 5.5 is the play.1 unit
The Read
→Stuff — 26% whiff, changeup 30% putaway, 9.9 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
→Command — 59% FPS, 3.4 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
✓Matchup — SEA K-rate runs +10% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #19/30 vs LHP); whiffs 31% on his changeup — a strong tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
→Volume — 5.6 IP avg — clears 5.5 with ~5.0 IP at his 9.9 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 6.4K vs 5.5 (+0.9K), σ1.8, CAP 60%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 6.4K vs 5.5. 60% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Bet wins if
✓SEA whiffs 31% on his changeup — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CH finishes counts (30% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓SEA K-rate runs +10% vs neutral for his mix — more K-prone than average, worth ~0.6K over a neutral draw
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~5.0 IP to reach 5.5, right at his 5.6 IP avg — one short outing and it misses

The Read
✓Stuff — 29% whiff, slider 34% putaway, 8.8 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
→Command — 60% FPS, 3.5 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
✓Matchup — NYM K-rate runs +5% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #15/30 vs RHP); whiffs 35% on his slider — a mild tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
→Volume — 5.3 IP avg — clears 4.5 with ~4.6 IP at his 8.8 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 5.2K vs 4.5 (+0.7K), σ2.3, CAP 58%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Marginal over edge (5.2K vs 4.5), 58% conviction — take it reduced.
Bet wins if
✓NYM whiffs 35% on his slider — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓SL finishes counts (34% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓NYM K-rate runs +5% vs neutral for his mix — more K-prone than average, worth ~0.3K over a neutral draw

① Signal
Signal
Lean over 7.5 — size down.0.5 unit
The Read
✓Stuff — 32% whiff, changeup 37% putaway, 10.7 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
✓Command — 69% FPS, 1.2 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
⚠Matchup — KC K-rate runs -8% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #6/30 vs LHP); whiffs 27% on his changeup — a strong headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
⚠Volume — 5.8 IP avg — short outings; clears 7.5 with ~6.3 IP at his 10.7 K/9 pace, so he must go deeper than usual.
✓Projection — 8.3K vs 7.5 (+0.8K), σ2.0, CAP 57%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 7.5 alt line, not the 6.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Marginal over edge (8.3K vs 7.5), 57% conviction — take it reduced.
Bet wins if
✓KC whiffs 27% on his changeup — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CH finishes counts (37% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓1.2 BB/9 — no free passes; he attacks and every PA is a K chance
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~6.3 IP to reach 7.5, right at his 5.8 IP avg — one short outing and it misses
✗KC K-rate runs -8% vs neutral for his mix — a contact draw that costs him ~0.5K

Signal
Under 4.5 is the play.1 unit
The Read
✓Stuff — 21% whiff, slider 24% putaway, 6.9 K/9 — below-average swing-and-miss; pitches to contact, not a K arm.
⚠Command — 4.4 BB/9 — walks stretch innings and PAs, which can feed late Ks against the under.
✓Matchup — SF K-rate runs -10% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #3/30 vs RHP); whiffs 30% on his slider — a strong K-suppressing lineup for his arsenal; reinforces the under.
✓Volume — 4.4 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
✓Projection — 2.8K vs 4.5 (-1.7K), σ1.9, CAP 78%, 8 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 4.0 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Strong under signal — 85% model probability below 4.5 at 2.8K projected.
Bet wins if
✓Would need ~5.9 IP to reach 4.5 — beyond his 4.4 IP avg, so length alone won't get there
✓6.9 K/9 — he simply doesn't miss enough bats to threaten 4.5
✓21% whiff on a slider-heavy mix — batters put it in play, few swing-through Ks
Bet loses if
✗6.9 K/9 is enough to reach 4.5 in a long outing

CAP 74% · implied 58.0% (-138) · model 60% (-150) · +2.0% edge
L6.5
-138
① Signal
Signal
Lean under 6.5 — size down.0.5 unit
The Read
✓Stuff — 20% whiff, changeup 24% putaway, 7.2 K/9 — below-average swing-and-miss; pitches to contact, not a K arm.
→Command — 63% FPS, 2.3 BB/9 — average count control; neutral for the under.
⚠Matchup — LAA K-rate runs +10% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #29/30 vs RHP); whiffs 33% on his changeup — a strong tailwind; this lineup could push him toward the number.
⚠Volume — 6.3 IP avg — goes deep, the under's main risk: more PAs = more K chances (needs ~8.1 IP to reach 6.5).
✓Projection — 4.9K vs 6.5 (-1.6K), σ1.7, CAP 74%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 6.5 alt line, not the 4.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Under edge present (4.9K vs 6.5), 74% confidence — lean only.
Bet wins if
✓Would need ~8.1 IP to reach 6.5 — beyond his 6.3 IP avg, so length alone won't get there
✓7.2 K/9 — he simply doesn't miss enough bats to threaten 6.5
✓20% whiff on a changeup-heavy mix — batters put it in play, few swing-through Ks
Bet loses if
✗LAA K-rate runs +10% vs neutral for his mix — swing-happy enough to feed cheap Ks
✗6.3 IP avg — deep outings hand him extra PAs to climb toward 6.5

CAP 77% · implied 61.2% (-158) · model 65% (-188) · +4.0% edge
L5.5
-158
① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
⚠Stuff — 30% whiff, changeup 35% putaway, 9.5 K/9 — he can miss bats, so the under leans on the line and matchup, not his stuff.
⚠Command — 4.4 BB/9 — walks stretch innings and PAs, which can feed late Ks against the under.
✓Matchup — TB K-rate runs -16% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #3/30 vs LHP); whiffs 26% on his changeup — a strong K-suppressing lineup for his arsenal; reinforces the under.
✓Volume — 5.0 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
⚠Volatility × matchup — σ2.6 × oppK 19.4 → −1.50K — a volatile K profile against a contact-leaning lineup. This exact combo historically over-projects (−1.5K bias in that cell), so the projection is trimmed 1.5K — already reflected in the PROJ, edge and tier above.
⚠Projection — 3.6K vs 5.5 (-1.9K), σ2.6, CAP 77%, 10 starts — real edge, but the floor is live (high variance).
→Line — best value at the 5.5 alt line, not the 4.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Model projects 3.6K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓Would need ~5.2 IP to reach 5.5 — beyond his 5.0 IP avg, so length alone won't get there
✓TB K-rate runs -16% vs neutral for his mix — a naturally K-suppressing lineup
✓5.0 IP avg — modest length caps his total K opportunity
Bet loses if
✗9.5 K/9 — the rate exists to reach 5.5 on a deep outing or if the lineup expands the zone
✗30% whiff — if he's sharp, Ks stack even against a contact lineup
✗σ2.6K — one high-K blowup start can clear 5.5 on its own

① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
⚠Stuff — 21% whiff, slider 24% putaway, 7.6 K/9 — below-average whiff; needs the matchup to carry the Ks.
⚠Command — 4.4 BB/9 — free passes eat PAs and pitch count; a real drag on the over.
⚠Matchup — STL K-rate runs -4% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #16/30 vs RHP); whiffs 26% on his slider — a mild headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
→Volume — 4.2 IP avg — clears 2.5 with ~3.0 IP at his 7.6 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
⚠Volatility × matchup — σ2.4 × oppK 21.9 → −0.25K — a volatile K profile against a contact-leaning lineup. This exact combo historically over-projects (−1.5K bias in that cell), so the projection is trimmed 0.2K — already reflected in the PROJ, edge and tier above.
✓Projection — 3.3K vs 2.5 (+0.8K), σ2.4, CAP 61%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 2.5 alt line, not the 4.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Model projects 3.3K vs 2.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~3.0 IP to hit 2.5 at his 7.6 K/9 — his 4.2 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓SL finishes counts (24% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗4.4 BB/9 — walks burn pitch count and cut his effective innings before any leash

CAP 55% · implied 68.3% (-215) · model 56% (-129) · -12.0% edge
L3.5
-215
① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
→Stuff — 26% whiff, curveball 30% putaway, 8.2 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
→Command — 58% FPS, 2.8 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
→Matchup — HOU K-rate runs -2% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #19/30 vs RHP); whiffs 34% on his curveball — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
→Volume — 5.0 IP avg — clears 3.5 with ~3.8 IP at his 8.2 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 4.0K vs 3.5 (+0.5K), σ2.0, CAP 55%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 3.5 alt line, not the 5.0 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Model projects 4.0K vs 3.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~3.8 IP to hit 3.5 at his 8.2 K/9 — his 5.0 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓HOU whiffs 34% on his curveball — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CU finishes counts (30% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗Start-to-start variance (σ2.0K) — the projection is a median, not a floor

① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
✓Stuff — 29% whiff, slider 35% putaway, 8.7 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
⚠Command — 4.6 BB/9 — free passes eat PAs and pitch count; a real drag on the over.
→Matchup — BOS K-rate runs +1% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #20/30 vs RHP); whiffs 37% on his slider — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
→Volume — 5.6 IP avg — clears 4.5 with ~4.7 IP at his 8.7 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 5.0K vs 4.5 (+0.5K), σ2.2, CAP 55%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Model projects 5.0K vs 4.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓BOS whiffs 37% on his slider — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓SL finishes counts (35% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~4.7 IP to reach 4.5, right at his 5.6 IP avg — one short outing and it misses
✗4.6 BB/9 — walks burn pitch count and cut his effective innings before any leash

The Read
→Stuff — 26% whiff, curveball 30% putaway, 9.0 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
⚠Command — 5.0 BB/9 — free passes eat PAs and pitch count; a real drag on the over.
⚠Matchup — CWS K-rate runs -6% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #26/30 vs RHP); whiffs 24% on his curveball — a mild headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
→Volume — 5.1 IP avg — clears 4.5 with ~4.5 IP at his 9.0 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 4.9K vs 4.5 (+0.4K), σ2.0, CAP 54%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Model projects 4.9K vs 4.5 — edge too thin to take a side.

✓CU finishes counts (30% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if

✗5.0 BB/9 — walks burn pitch count and cut his effective innings before any leash

CAP 54% · implied 60.8% (-155) · model 56% (-125) · -5.1% edge
L4.5
-155
① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
⚠Stuff — 21% whiff, sweeper 25% putaway, 9.2 K/9 — below-average whiff; needs the matchup to carry the Ks.
⚠Command — 55% FPS — falls behind too often; weak contact tends to follow, not Ks.
→Matchup — LAD K-rate runs -2% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #7/30 vs LHP); whiffs 36% on his sweeper — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
→Volume — 5.2 IP avg — clears 4.5 with ~4.4 IP at his 9.2 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 4.9K vs 4.5 (+0.4K), σ1.1, CAP 54%, 7 starts — tight, reliable projection; not one-start noise.
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Model projects 4.9K vs 4.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓LAD whiffs 36% on his sweeper — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓SW finishes counts (25% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓σ1.1K — tight game-to-game spread, so 4.9K is a dependable center
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~4.4 IP to reach 4.5, right at his 5.2 IP avg — one short outing and it misses

① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
→Stuff — 22% whiff, sweeper 26% putaway, 8.7 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
→Command — 60% FPS, 2.9 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
✓Matchup — CIN K-rate runs +15% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #30/30 vs RHP); whiffs 30% on his sweeper — a strong tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
⚠Volume — 4.9 IP avg — short outings; clears 5.5 with ~5.7 IP at his 8.7 K/9 pace, so he must go deeper than usual.
⚠Projection — 5.8K vs 5.5 (+0.3K), σ2.7, CAP 52%, 10 starts — real edge, but the floor is live (high variance).
Verdict: Model projects 5.8K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓CIN whiffs 30% on his sweeper — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓SW finishes counts (26% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓CIN K-rate runs +15% vs neutral for his mix — more K-prone than average, worth ~0.7K over a neutral draw
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~5.7 IP to reach 5.5, right at his 4.9 IP avg — one short outing and it misses
✗σ2.7K — floor sits near 3K, under 5.5 on an off night

The Read
✓Stuff — 29% whiff, slider 34% putaway, 8.8 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
✓Command — 65% FPS, 2.4 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
✓Matchup — PIT K-rate runs +9% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #29/30 vs LHP); whiffs 35% on his slider — a strong tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
⚠Volume — 5.2 IP avg — short outings; clears 5.5 with ~5.6 IP at his 8.8 K/9 pace, so he must go deeper than usual.
✓Projection — 5.8K vs 5.5 (+0.3K), σ2.2, CAP 52%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Model projects 5.8K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.

✓PIT whiffs 35% on his slider — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit

CAP 52% · implied 56.5% (-130) · model 55% (-123) · -1.3% edge
L4.5
-130
① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
✓Stuff — 27% whiff, slider 32% putaway, 7.6 K/9 — solid swing-and-miss; the Ks are there when he pitches ahead.
⚠Command — 4.0 BB/9 — free passes eat PAs and pitch count; a real drag on the over.
✓Matchup — BAL K-rate runs +11% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #27/30 vs RHP); whiffs 37% on his slider — a strong tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
⚠Volume — 4.7 IP avg — short outings; clears 4.5 with ~5.3 IP at his 7.6 K/9 pace, so he must go deeper than usual.
✓Projection — 4.8K vs 4.5 (+0.3K), σ2.3, CAP 52%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Model projects 4.8K vs 4.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓BAL whiffs 37% on his slider — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓SL finishes counts (32% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓BAL K-rate runs +11% vs neutral for his mix — more K-prone than average, worth ~0.5K over a neutral draw
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~5.3 IP to reach 4.5, right at his 4.7 IP avg — one short outing and it misses
✗4.0 BB/9 — walks burn pitch count and cut his effective innings before any leash

CAP 34% · implied 54.3% (-119) · model 61% (-155) · +6.5% edge
L4.5
-119
① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
✓Stuff — 20% whiff, curveball 23% putaway, 6.3 K/9 — below-average swing-and-miss; pitches to contact, not a K arm.
→Command — 61% FPS, 3.2 BB/9 — average count control; neutral for the under.
✓Matchup — WSH K-rate runs -7% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #18/30 vs LHP); whiffs 33% on his curveball — a mild K-suppressing lineup for his arsenal; reinforces the under.
✓Volume — 5.6 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
✓Projection — 3.7K vs 4.5 (-0.8K), σ1.4, CAP 34%, 10 starts — tight, reliable projection; not one-start noise.
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 4.0 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Model projects 3.7K vs 4.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓Would need ~6.4 IP to reach 4.5 — beyond his 5.6 IP avg, so length alone won't get there
✓6.3 K/9 — he simply doesn't miss enough bats to threaten 4.5
✓20% whiff on a curveball-heavy mix — batters put it in play, few swing-through Ks
Bet loses if
✗6.3 K/9 is enough to reach 4.5 in a long outing

✓Volume — 5.1 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
✓Projection — 4.2K vs 5.5 (-1.3K), σ2.4, CAP 25%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.

The Case

FAIR-102✗
Forecast
5.7K +0.2K
σ2.2 · Moderate
CAP 50/100
Pois 0 · Sim 0
SIG 0/99 WEAK
▾
① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
✓Stuff — 28% whiff, slider 33% putaway, 9.0 K/9 — solid swing-and-miss; the Ks are there when he pitches ahead.
✓Command — 68% FPS, 2.5 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
✓Matchup — COL K-rate runs +7% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #27/30 vs LHP); whiffs 36% on his slider — a mild tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
⚠Volume — 5.1 IP avg — short outings; clears 5.5 with ~5.5 IP at his 9.0 K/9 pace, so he must go deeper than usual.
✓Projection — 5.7K vs 5.5 (+0.2K), σ2.2, CAP 50%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Model projects 5.7K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.

The Read
⚠Stuff — 18% whiff, splitter 21% putaway, 5.1 K/9 — below-average whiff; needs the matchup to carry the Ks.
→Command — 60% FPS, 2.3 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
→Matchup — MIL K-rate runs -1% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #11/30 vs RHP); whiffs 48% on his splitter — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
→Volume — 5.4 IP avg — clears 2.5 with ~4.4 IP at his 5.1 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 2.6K vs 2.5 (+0.1K), σ1.2, CAP 48%, 10 starts — tight, reliable projection; not one-start noise.
→Line — best value at the 2.5 alt line, not the 3.0 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Model projects 2.6K vs 2.5 — edge too thin to take a side.

StrategyNW–LWin%ROIΔ
All signals393238–15561%-0.3%—

No flag-avoidance subset clearly beats betting all signals yet (393 graded bets). Skipping flagged bets isn’t improving ROI at this sample.
How the flagged bets themselves did (what you’d skip)
FADE2111–1052%-3.0%
K-SUPP6745–2267%+5.7%
HIGH-σ6441–2364%+5.1%
ALT8254–2866%+3.5%
LEASH72–529%-62.6%
THIN136–746%-37.1%
σ-CUT119–282%+22.8%
σ-FADE22–0100%+45.1%
STALE42–250%-12.7%
Definitions · HIGH-σ σ ≥ 2.5K (start-to-start K spread) · FADE opp blended K < 21% · K-SUPP opp blended K 21–22.5% · LEASH injury-return or <6 starts & <5.5 IP avg · THIN <8 starts · σ-FADE σ>2 × opp K<23 (proj haircut) · ALT best line ≠ market main

AK · actual bets
+2.8%
ROI
$+812 on $28,523
199-126-5
record
61.2% win rate
+2.31%
CLV
beat close 88%
Not reproducible. These are AK’s real fills. He skips ~42% of board plays and bets books Ballpark Pal cannot see (Kalshi: 64 bets, +$571). A follower betting this board gets the Results tab number, not this one. The gap between them is execution, not model.
P&L
NET PROFIT+$811.92
staked$28,523.30
returned$29,335.22
record199–126–5  ·  61%
avg stake$85.40
Where the edge comes from
Each row is the same season, re-run under a different rule. They do not add up — each one replaces the row above it.
if you bet every board play at FanDuel-2.0%
… but you only took 334 of 334+2.3%
your picking  +4.3 pts
… and you shopped them across 11 books+2.8%
your shopping  +0.5 pts
= what you actually made+2.85%  $+812
The 0 you passed on would have gone -12.5%. Your picking is worth more than your shopping — but note you are not selecting on price: 43% of what you took cleared FAIR, and so did 42% of what you skipped.
Selection skill · took vs skipped
Both columns priced at FanDuel — the only difference is AK’s choice. ✓ his picks beat his passes.
TIERTOOKROISKIPROI
STRONG42+10.1%14-14.6%✓
MOD74-5.3%34-35.3%✓
LEAN OVER27-4.5%24-14.7%✓
LEAN UNDER26-28.7%61+7.3%✗
UNDER24+18.2%9-11.5%✓
LEAN UNDER runs backwards. The ones he takes go −28.7%; the ones he passes go +7.3%. Take the tier systematically or drop it — the current middle is worse than either. (n=26, small — do not overreact.)
By book
BOOKBETSSTAKEDP/LROICLV
Kalshi93$9,000$+524+5.8%+2.04%
OnyxOdds62$4,425$-589-13.3%+2.32%
NoVig51$4,306$-74-1.7%+3.02%
Fanatics38$4,000$+686+17.2%+1.63%
Bet10530$2,375$+317+13.4%+1.34%
FanDuel26$2,375$-175-7.4%+2.15%
ProphetX20$1,268$-32-2.5%+2.74%
Caesars6$375$+42+11.2%+2.03%
Rebet4$200$+47+23.5%+6.14%
Fliff2$75$+61+81.0%+0.00%
BookMaker2$125$+4+3.2%+3.38%
Book ROI is confounded — it reflects which bets landed where, not book quality. CLV is the honest column, and it is a consistent +2 to +3% everywhere.
MODEL
v9.9 · how it works · how to use it · where it fails · through 2026-07-24
1 · How accurate is it, really?
The only benchmark that matters is the number you are betting against. So here is our projection scored against the sportsbook’s own posted line, on all 552 pitcher-starts we projected (not just the ones bet), using the same measure — average miss, in strikeouts.
This model1.91 K
average miss, in strikeouts
The book’s posted line1.90 K
the number you are betting against
Guessing the league average2.06 K
the do-nothing baseline
One caveat on the bars above. 98% of book lines are half-numbers (5.5, not 6), so a line can never land exactly on the actual — it carries a built-in ½-strikeout miss by design. Our projection is a point, which can. The two effects roughly cancel (the line is also the market consensus, which helps it), which is why the averages land so close — but read “we match the line” as directional, not a clean dead heat.
We forecast about as well as the market — not better. There is no prediction edge here, and this board will never claim one.
+0.13 K
our bias
±0.20 at 95% — indistinguishable from zero
+0.34 K
the book’s bias
the posted line sits slightly BELOW where outcomes land
What bias means: the average gap between what happened and what was predicted. Zero is the target — it means no systematic lean in either direction.

Our projection is even-handed (+0.13, statistically zero). The book’s line carries a real lean (+0.34): actuals came in above the posted number on average. That is not the book forecasting badly — a betting line is shaded by where the money sits and by the vig, not set to be an unbiased average. But it does mean the posted number is slightly low relative to outcomes, while ours is centred.
2 · The record
Every signal this board has posted, graded at real prices. Losing tiers shown alongside winning ones.
TIERSTAKEW–LWIN%ROIP&L
STRONG2u · 5838–2065.5%+1.6%+1.85u
MOD1u · 13179–5260.3%-2.5%-3.28u
LEAN OVER0.5u · 6738–2956.7%-3.0%-0.98u
UNDER1u · 4429–1565.9%+7.0%+3.09u
LEAN UNDER0.5u · 9354–3958.1%-1.8%-0.80u
OVERALL393 bets238–15560.6%-0.0%-0.12u
Staked 2u / 1u / 0.5u by tier. ROI uses the best price we found across the books we track.
Read the table again, carefully.
MOD wins 60.3% of the time and still loses 2.5%.

That is not a broken forecast. That is a sound forecast bought at a bad price. A 60.6% win rate loses money if you routinely pay −250.

This is the single most important thing to understand about this board — which is why it is printed on every card. Read on.
3 · How to read a card
Four things decide whether a play is worth making.
TIER → how much to stake
STRONG 2u   MOD 1u   LEAN OVER 0.5u   UNDER 1u   LEAN UNDER 0.5u
Conviction, not certainty. STRONG has been right 65.5% of the time — which means it has been wrong nearly a third of the time.
P(win) → how often the model expects it to hit
Calibrated against our own graded results, not a raw model output. Capped at 75%, because we have never actually observed a bucket win more often than that.
FAIR → what the price should be
P(win) written as odds. FAIR −231 while you are offered −190 means you are buying below fair value. Offered −300, you are overpaying — even if the pick is sound.
✓ CLEARS → the price is actually worth it
The most important badge on the board. It appears only when P(win) beats the break-even of the price on offer.

Signals that cleared returned +10.8%. Signals that did not returned −10.8%. Same model, same projections — the only difference is what you paid.

Typically about 1 in 4 clear. The board still posts all of them, because the price you can reach is not always the price we can see.
4 · How to actually use this
1. Shop the price. Every card names the book with the best number we found. On a typical slate, FanDuel holds the best price on fewer than one rung in three. The same bet at two books is often the difference between +EV and −EV.

2. Weight the CLEARS. If you want the version of this board with the strongest historical record, lean on the green badge.

3. Respect the tier sizing. STRONG is 2u for a reason. Flat-betting every signal throws away the model’s best information — its ranking.

4. Do not chase the long alternate lines. They look like value and the model is least reliable exactly there. See section 7.
5 · What the model actually does
No black box — and no formula either. Here is the honest shape of it.
Recent form, adjusted for who he faced
The last ten starts, each re-scaled for that opponent’s strikeout rate. Seven Ks against a contact team is a better start than seven against a whiffing one, and the model treats it that way.
Stuff and command
Whiff rate, putaway rate, first-pitch-strike rate — each measured against league average. A pitcher who misses bats and works ahead turns more plate appearances into strikeouts.
Tonight’s specific lineup
Not the team’s season average — the nine batters actually posted, and how each of them fares against this pitcher’s particular pitch mix.
Volatility
Some arms are metronomes; some swing from 3 Ks to 11. The more erratic the arm, the less the model trusts recent form and the more it leans on the underlying rate.
6 · Every number on the card
TERMWHAT IT ISHOW TO USE IT
projThe model’s projected strikeouts for this start.The headline number. Its distance from the line is the edge.
edgeproj − the line being bet, in strikeouts.How far the model sits from the book. A big edge at a bad price is still a bad bet.
edge% (ep)edge ÷ line × 100 — the edge as a share of the line.Stops +1.5K on a 2.5 line (huge) counting the same as +1.5K on a 9.5 line (modest). Every tier has a floor on it.
CAPA probability (0–1). Raw P(over), shrunk toward 50% for three kinds of doubt: volatility, small sample, and inputs that disagree with each other.This is the gate every tier is judged on. High CAP means the model is confident and its inputs agree.
σ (sigma)Standard deviation of the last 10 K totals. Volatility.Low = metronome. High = swings from 3 Ks to 11. High σ widens outcomes and lowers CAP.
P(win)The calibrated chance this bet hits — fitted against our own graded history, capped at 75%.The probability to trust. FAIR and CLEARS are both built from it.
FAIRP(win) written as American odds. The model’s fair price.FAIR −231 while you are offered −190 → you are buying below fair value. Offered −300 → you are overpaying.
Implied%The price you actually pay, expressed as the win rate it requires to break even.The same comparison as FAIR, in probability units instead of odds.
CLEARSGreen badge. Appears only when P(win) beats Implied%.The most predictive marker on this board. Clearing signals ran +10.8%; non-clearing ran −10.8%. Roughly 1 in 4 clear.
best_lineThe rung actually being bet — not always the posted main line.Edge, CAP and tier are ALL computed on this. When it differs from the main line the card says so.
MIX-K%The average strikeout rate of tonight’s nine batters, weighted against this pitcher’s pitch mix.The lineup-specific half of the opponent factor. Blended 50/50 with the team’s season K% versus that handedness.
OPPEASY / HARD verdict on the opposing lineup.EASY = they whiff. HARD = contact team, and the model’s weakest ground. About a 5pp spread end to end.
MATCHPitcher’s whiff% on a pitch, scaled by how vulnerable this lineup is to that pitch.Context only — it does not feed the projection. A great slider means less against a lineup that does not chase sliders.
L10 Raw / AdjLast-10 K average, raw and then re-scaled for the quality of opponents faced.Adj is the one that matters. 7 Ks against a contact team beats 7 against a whiffing one.
7 · The warning flags
These appear when something about the matchup needs a second look.
FLAGWHAT IT MEANSHOW TO USE IT
σ-CUTThe projection was trimmed. Fires when σ>2.0 AND the opponent sits 1.7pp below that hand’s league K% — a volatile arm into a contact lineup, where the model provably over-projects (measured bias −1.48K). Hand-relative since 18 Jul 2026 (RHP 23.0, LHP 23.6); the old fixed 23.0 was hand-blind and under-fired for lefties.The card shows the size of the cut. −0.9K changes the bet; −0.1K is rounding.
LEASHWorkload ceiling. Recent innings and pitch counts suggest he may not go deep enough to reach the line.A high projection is worth little if he is pulled in the 5th.
HARD MATCHOpponent is top-8 hardest to strike out against this pitcher’s handedness. Overs only. Replaced the old absolute FADE/K-SUPP cutoffs on 18 Jul 2026 — those were hand-blind and separated outcomes barely better than chance (eff<22.5: 55.9%/−4.0% vs eff≥22.5: 59.4%/−2.3%).Backtest, 206 graded overs: 45.2%, ROI −25.8% (n=42). Reduce size or pass. Advisory — tier and units are unchanged.
HARD+σTop-8 hard matchup and σ>2.0 — a volatile arm against a lineup that does not strike out.Worst cell measured: 40.0%, ROI −32.2% (n=20). Dropping this cell alone flips the graded over book from −6.55u to +0.38u — though that is in-sample, so it is tracked live before being wired in.
K-SUPPOpponent ranks 9–15 hardest vs this hand — a moderately strikeout-suppressing lineup.Backtest: 51.2%, ROI −14.1% (n=43). Demand a larger edge.
HIGH-σVolatile arm (σ > 2).Wider range of outcomes. Size accordingly.
THINFewer than 8 starts in the sample.Lower confidence. CAP already penalises it.
ALTThe bet sits on an alternate rung, not the posted main line.Make sure you are betting the number the card names.
8 · Where it fails
Volatile arms against contact lineups. The model over-projects here, consistently. Cards carrying σ-CUT have already been trimmed for it, and it remains the weakest matchup on the board.

The long alternate rungs. The further above the main line you climb, the more the model disagrees with the market — and the market is usually right. Those rungs are the model’s worst-calibrated region. They will look like value. Be sceptical.

Paying juice. Worth repeating, because it costs the most: this board wins 60.6% of its bets and still loses money at bad prices. The pick is only half the bet.
Every figure on this page is recomputed from the live results ledger on each build. Nothing is hand-entered and nothing is back-fitted. If a tier goes underwater, you will see it here first.
↑
